steps.title: Enter the scatterplot as a table
steps.content: Use Desmos for the model fit. Reading from the grid, the data are approximately $(0,80)$, $(1,90)$, $(2,61)$, $(3,66)$, $(4,51)$, $(5,44)$, $(6,25)$, $(7,25)$, and $(8,0)$.

steps.title: Fit a line
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$. The regression gives a slope close to $-10$ and a $y$-intercept close to $90$. That points to $y=90-10x$.

steps.title: Check against the choices
steps.content: Choices B and C have slope $-0.1$, which is much too flat for the data. Choice A has slope $-10$ but starts at $80$, too low for the overall trend. Choice D, $y=90-10x$, matches the regression and gives $y=50$ at $x=4$, right near the middle of the scatterplot. Choice D is correct.
